skill.name: zach-search-term-report-analyzer
skill.description: Analyze Amazon Ads search term reports (SP, SB, SD) to identify wasteful terms, scale-up candidates, attribute words, scenario words, and trend changes for informed bid, negative keyword, and listing optimization decisions.
skill.summary: Turn Amazon Ads search term reports into actionable negative keyword, bid control, and scale-up recommendations.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 448 potential issues, but the vast majority are false positives from markdown formatting (backticks in .md files, relative path references) and test fixture strings (sample HTML in test files). The only real code-level findings involve subprocess.run with hardcoded arguments (no injection risk) and HTTP requests to Amazon product pages (legitimate web scraping with proper User-Agent headers). No prompt injection attempts, no data exfiltration patterns, and no malicious intent detected. The skill clearly documents its boundaries, does not modify ad accounts directly, and provides an opt-out flag for live fetching.

low_findings.title: Subprocess invocation for HTTP fetching
low_findings.description: scripts/browser_utils.py uses subprocess.run to invoke curl for fetching Amazon product pages. Arguments are passed as a list (not shell=True), so there is no command injection risk. The script includes a captcha detection mechanism and a throttle function to respect rate limits. This is a standard web scraping pattern with appropriate safeguards.

low_findings.title: Optional live Amazon page fetching
low_findings.description: scripts/fetch_listing_context.py can fetch live Amazon product listing pages to extract title, bullets, and description for context. The skill provides a --skip-live-listing-fetch flag to disable this. Only the public Amazon product page is accessed (no authentication, no credentials). This is a legitimate data enrichment step for the search term analysis workflow.

content.user_title: Analyze Amazon Search Term Reports Like a Pro
content.value_statement: Amazon sellers waste ad budget on irrelevant search terms and miss scale-up opportunities hidden in their PPC data. This skill transforms raw SP, SB, and SD search term reports into clear negative keyword candidates, bid control targets, and Listing feedback insights, all without modifying your ad account.
actual_capabilities: Classify search terms into brand, competitor, ASIN, core category, attribute, scenario, irrelevant, and uncertain categories
Calculate 7-day, 14-day, and 30-day rolling metrics for impressions, clicks, spend, orders, CVR, ACOS, and ROAS
Identify negative keyword candidates based on spend thresholds and conversion performance
Detect scale-up candidates that exceed the ASIN baseline CVR or ACOS
Extract attribute words and scenario words from search terms for Listing feedback
Generate a structured markdown report, CSV detail table, anomaly list, and JSON run summary
limitations: Does not directly modify Amazon Ads bids, budgets, negative keywords, or campaign structure
Cannot function if core fields (date, search term, clicks, spend) are missing from the report
Optional live Listing fetch may be blocked by Amazon captcha; a local context file can be provided as fallback
Analysis is per-ASIN; multiple ASINs in one report require user selection before proceeding

use_cases.title: Weekly PPC optimization for Amazon sellers
use_cases.description: A solo Amazon FBA seller wants to review their weekly Sponsored Products search term report and decide which terms to negate, which to reduce bids on, and which to scale. They upload the CSV and receive a structured report with clear action labels.
use_cases.target_user: Amazon FBA sellers and PPC specialists

use_cases.title: Listing optimization feedback from real search language
use_cases.description: A brand manager wants to understand what attribute words and use-case scenarios real shoppers use. The skill surfaces recurring attribute and scenario words from search terms, which can inform title, bullet, and A+ content updates.
use_cases.target_user: Amazon brand managers and listing copywriters

use_cases.title: Multi-format report support for agencies
use_cases.description: An agency managing multiple ASINs receives reports in different formats from different clients. The skill auto-detects report type (SP, SB, SD) and supports CSV, XLSX, XLSM, and XLS input files, standardizing them into a unified analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Basic search term report analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Amazon Sponsored Products search term report. Here is the file: [upload CSV]. Identify negative keyword candidates, scale-up opportunities, and any attribute or scenario words I should consider for my Listing.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user uploading a weekly SP report

prompt_templates.title: Targeted negative keyword extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: From this search term report [upload file], extract only terms that have spent more than $10 with zero orders. List them as negative keyword candidates with their total spend and clicks.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to focus purely on waste reduction

prompt_templates.title: Scale-up candidate identification
prompt_templates.prompt: My target ACOS is 25%. From this report [upload file], find search terms that have CVR above the ASIN average and ACOS below 25%, with at least 3 orders. Output them as scale-up candidates with confidence levels.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to identify expansion opportunities with specific thresholds

prompt_templates.title: Listing feedback from search language
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the search terms in this report [upload file] and extract the top 20 attribute words and top 10 scenario words. Group them by frequency and suggest which ones could be added to my product title or bullets.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants Listing content improvement ideas from real shopper language

output_examples.input: Upload a Sponsored Products search term report CSV with columns: Search Term, Impressions, Clicks, Spend, Orders, Sales.
output: | Search Term | Spend | Clicks | Orders | Action |
| Search Term | Spend | Orders | CVR | ACOS | Action |

output_examples.input: Upload a report and ask specifically for attribute and scenario words for Listing feedback.

best_practices: Always provide a local Listing context file with --listing-context-file to avoid captcha issues and ensure consistent analysis across runs
Set --target-acos explicitly to get more reliable scale-up and reduce-bid recommendations instead of relying on report baselines alone
Review the anomaly list CSV alongside the main report, as it flags conflicting 7/14/30-day signals that need human judgment
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to directly modify your Amazon Ads account — it only produces recommendations that require manual review and execution
Do not upload reports with mixed ASINs without selecting a target ASIN first — the analysis is per-ASIN and mixing them will produce unreliable results
Do not rely solely on the scale-up candidates without checking the confidence level — low-confidence suggestions may need additional data before acting

faq.question: What report formats are supported?
faq.answer: CSV, XLSX, XLSM, and XLS files are supported. The skill auto-detects the report type (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, or Sponsored Display) based on column headers.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my Amazon Ads account?
faq.answer: No. The skill only produces analysis reports and recommendations. All bid changes, negative keyword additions, and budget adjustments must be done manually in the Amazon Ads console after reviewing the output.

faq.question: What if Amazon blocks the live Listing page fetch?
faq.answer: The skill detects captcha and fetch failures and will not block the analysis. You can also use the --skip-live-listing-fetch flag and provide a local context file instead via --listing-context-file.

faq.question: Can I analyze multiple ASINs in one report?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes one ASIN at a time. If multiple ASINs are detected, it will list them as candidates and ask you to select one before proceeding.

faq.question: What output files are generated?
faq.answer: Four files: a main markdown report, a CSV detail table, a CSV anomaly list, and a JSON run summary. All are saved to outputs/search-term-report-analyzer/ with a date and ASIN in the filename.

faq.question: How are confidence levels assigned to recommendations?
faq.answer: Confidence is based on data volume (clicks and orders), consistency across 7/14/30-day windows, and how clearly the term matches a known category. High confidence means strong data and clear signals; low confidence means the term needs more data or manual review.
